description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined fuselage with a two-tone color scheme, primarily light gray on the upper surfaces and dark blue on the lower. It is equipped with a large main rotor system consisting of six long, slender blades with red and white tips, and a smaller tail rotor with four blades, also marked with red and white. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple large, slightly tinted windows providing wide visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two robust, dark blue sponsons on either side, likely housing retractable wheels or floats. The tail boom extends rearward, supporting the tail rotor and a horizontal stabilizer, and the helicopter displays subtle markings and insignia on its body. No visible registration number is present on the exterior.
description: A large, two-tone gray and blue helicopter with six main rotor blades, a four-blade tail rotor, and a spacious cockpit with multiple windows. It features robust side sponsons for landing and a streamlined tail boom.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, six main rotor blades, side sponsons, and the tail boom with the tail rotor clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail rotor, horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetrical placement of the side sponsons.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the main rotor, cockpit, and tail boom from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the cockpit windows, nose, and the arrangement of the main rotor blades above.
attribute_rotor_blades: Six long, narrow main rotor blades made of composite material, each with a black base color and red and white stripes at the tips. The blades are evenly spaced and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Four-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, with each blade featuring red and white stripes at the tips. The rotor is exposed and lacks a protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter does not have traditional skids; instead, it features two large, dark blue sponsons on either side, likely housing retractable wheels or floats for landing.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, rectangular cockpit windows wrap around the front and sides, slightly tinted and framed in black, providing wide visibility for the pilots.
